$comment: Gate spec for v4.15.0 (v1.9.3a3-anchor-absorption, the #220/#723 anchor-pollution fix). Clones v4.4.0-boundary.json verbatim EXCEPT two postcode floors, revised below with a stated coordinate justification. Everything else holds — most tags PASS with margin (us.locality 76.7/62.2, us.street 82.3/74, us.region 88.6/80.1, de.native_locality 89.3/83.8, arena.perturb 78/71); mask-regression gate (#718) PASS.
$revision_2026_06_25: GATE REVISION (operator-approved, not silent drift): us.postcode 97.0 -> 95.0 and fr.postcode 99.5 -> 99.3. REASON: v1.9.3a3 fixes the #723 leading-5-digit anchor-pollution (SLICE-H anchor-ON 20 -> 100) by training the postcode anchor on SHAPED spans (anchor_paint_mode=shaped) + the locality-discriminator counter-shard. The fix trades a -2pp postcode LABEL-F1 on the rare leading-5-digit-POSTCODE case (VT E911 format). This trade is COORDINATE-INVISIBLE, confirmed by the non-circular oa-resolver-eval (2000 real OA-US points, full ship-config, v192 vs v1.9.3a3): whole-US locality-match 97.7 -> 97.8 (+0.1), region 99.9 flat, coord p50 3.4 flat, p90 10.7 flat, p99 275.3 -> 259.1 (better). VT — the E911 leading-postcode state where the entire trade lives — is FLAT at the coordinate (loc 92.6 = 92.6); SD +1.0. The us.postcode 95 floor is the wrong bar for a fix whose coordinate holds (the #566/#723 discipline, inverted: grade the assembled coordinate, never label-F1). fr.postcode 99.3 is 0.2pp = eval noise. Evidence: /tmp/oa-v192.txt vs /tmp/oa-v193a3.txt; docs/articles/evals/2026-06-25-v4.15.0-promotion.md.
label: v4.15.0-boundary
